news.title: Prestige Estates records ₹6,579 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Prestige Estates recorded pre-sales of ₹6,579 crore in Q1 FY27, with sales volume of 6.04 million sq ft and 3,337 units sold. Collections stood at ₹4,802 crore during the quarter.

news.title: Delhi development body's revenue jumps 120% in Q1 FY27
news.para: Delhi Development Authority housing sales reached Rs 1,020 crores in the first quarter. This revenue marks a significant increase from the previous financial year's sales. Narela emerged as the top contributor, selling 1,153 flats during this period. Infrastructure development and improved connectivity are supporting this housing growth. Ongoing schemes are available online on a first-come, first-served basis.

news.title: Imperial Holding Group’s long-term vision for luxury real estate in India
news.para: India's luxury real estate market sees a shift towards wellness and lifestyle. Affluent buyers now prioritize privacy and quality of life in their homes. Imperial Holding Group is developing premium mountain communities like Auramah Valley. These developments focus on wellness, nature, and community for residents. The company aims to build destinations offering sustainable value and long-term appreciation.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government plans to issue property cards for assets in urban areas
news.para: Andhra Pradesh plans to introduce property cards for all urban properties. This reform aims to create a single, authenticated record of ownership. The initiative will integrate revenue, registration, and municipal records into one digital document. Detailed surveys using modern technologies will prepare accurate digital records for issuance. This system will simplify transactions and prevent fraudulent ownership claims.

news.title: HC asks Uttar Pradesh government to consider grievance forum for apartment owner-RWA disputes
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has directed the Uttar Pradesh government to consider establishing a dedicated forum. This forum will address disputes between apartment owners and their Residents Welfare Associations. The court noted a lack of effective mechanisms for resolving such internal association conflicts. It dismissed a petition challenging RWA decisions on gate closures and parking charges. The ruling highlights the need for a specialized body to handle these common issues.

news.title: Aurum PropTech to acquire Locon Solutions for ₹458 crore
news.para: Aurum PropTech’s board has approved the acquisition of 100% stake in Locon Solutions, owner of Housing(dot)com, from REA India. The transaction will be carried out through a share swap involving preferential allotment of 1.98 crore equity shares.

news.title: Real estate capital inflows rise 32% to $8.5 billion in H1 2026: Report
news.para: India’s real estate sector recorded equity capital inflows of $8.5 billion in H1 2026, up 32% year-on-year, according to CBRE. Domestic investors accounted for about 92% of total inflows in Q2 2026.

news.title: Chennai civic body extends special property tax collection camps till July 31
news.para: Chennai Corporation extends property tax collection camps until July 31.  Nearly half of property owners have not yet paid their dues.  The civic body aims to recover pending property tax through intensive collection efforts.  Property tax is a vital revenue source for essential civic works.  Owners are urged to pay by month-end to avoid penal interest.

news.title: Housing project stalled for 12 years, consumer panel orders YEIDA to refund flat buyer
news.para: A consumer commission ordered Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority to refund Rs 2.8 lakh.  The authority failed to deliver a flat under its 2014 housing scheme.  Buyer Omveer Singh sought a refund after the scheme did not materialize.  YEIDA argued Singh defaulted on payments, but the commission disagreed.  The authority must now pay the registration fee with interest and litigation costs.

news.title: Jaypee deal to help Dalmia Bharat have pan-India presence: MD
news.para: Dalmia Bharat plans significant pan-India expansion, primarily through organic growth. The company is also open to strategic acquisitions to accelerate market entry. Recent acquisitions have boosted capacity, with further scaling planned by FY28 and FY31. Greenfield projects and capacity enhancements are underway, including a major plant in Kadapa. Dalmia Bharat aims to strengthen its position in key demand hotspots across India.

news.title: Telangana HC halts GHMC move to demolish BLISS residential project in Somajiguda
news.para: The Telangana High Court granted temporary relief to Pradeep Constructions on Wednesday.  GHMC was ordered not to take any coercive action against the BLISS residential project.  The civic body had issued demolition notices to the developer on July seventh.  The court posted the matter for further hearing on July twenty-third.  Authorities were directed not to take coercive action until the next hearing.

news.title: Uttarakhand HC sets aside DM’s order granting fire NOC to Dehradun builder
news.para: The Uttarakhand High Court quashed a district magistrate's order directing fire safety NOC issuance. This order was for Pinnacle Residency despite identified fire safety deficiencies and unauthorized construction. The court found the magistrate's reliance on a builder's video legally unsustainable. Authorities should have obtained an expert fire safety audit instead of video evidence. The matter was remanded for re-evaluation, ensuring adherence to safety guidelines.

news.title: Transfer of redevelopment rights cannot be taxed as 'other income': ITAT
news.para: Mumbai ITAT ruled development rights transfer is capital gains. Taxpayers can claim Section 54EC exemption by investing in bonds. This ruling impacts numerous Mumbai redevelopment projects significantly. The tribunal directed tax officers to verify exemption compliance.  Such reinvestment reduces the taxable capital gains component.

news.title: Frasers Property-led consortium tops bid for Bayshore Drive site in Singapore at $1.6 billion
news.para: A consortium led by Frasers Property and FCT has secured the Bayshore Drive site. They submitted the highest bid of S$2.1 billion for this mixed-use development. The project will include residential units and commercial spaces, featuring a retail mall. This development offers FCT a new growth avenue and future acquisition opportunities. Frasers Property holds a significant stake in Frasers Centrepoint Trust.

news.title: SC to hear ED plea against transfer of Amrapali Group's money laundering case to Ghaziabad
news.para: The Supreme Court will hear the Enforcement Directorate's plea against a high court order.  This order transferred a money laundering case involving Amrapali group homebuyers' funds.  The Allahabad High Court had directed the case transfer to a Ghaziabad court.  The Enforcement Directorate argued the high court wrongly transferred the money laundering case.  Notices were issued to former promoters of the real estate firm.

news.title: Karnataka CM assures legal measures to address apartment owners' grievances
news.para: Highlighting problems related to property ownership, Shivakumar said purchasing an apartment should automatically make a buyer its rightful owner and noted that many residents continue to face difficulties in getting their properties registered.

news.title: Empty skyscrapers: China's property slump still throttling growth
news.para: The world's tallest unoccupied skyscraper was meant to boast a rooftop pool and a luxury hotel after completion, but instead it has stood deserted in the Chinese port city of Tianjin for years. The 595.5-metre (1,954-foot) skyscraper is emblematic of the years-long crisis that has left Chinese cities dotted with abandoned high-rises and residential towers.

news.title: Kalyan-Dombivli civic body fines Titwala township for failing to process waste
news.para: The Kalyan-Dombivli Municipal Corporation imposed a one lakh rupee penalty on Regency Sarvam township. This action followed repeated complaints about non-functional waste processing facilities within the complex. Civic officials found the in-house waste processing plant was not operational during an inspection. The township had previously received notices to restore its waste processing system. Strict action will continue against establishments failing to comply with waste management norms.
